description: Visibility-restricted retrieval MUST NOT distinguish 'not found' from 'not authorized' externally. An unauthorized requester for a restricted context MUST receive the same response as a requester asking for a non-existent ctx_id: HTTP 404 with error code 'not_found'. Returning 'visibility_denied' externally would leak the existence of the context to unauthorized parties. Per RFC-ACDP-0002 §7, RFC-ACDP-0004 §2.3, and RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5.

expected.notes: Producer is always in the effective audience for their own contexts.

scenarios.name: Authorized requester (in audience) requests restricted context
Unauthorized requester (NOT in audience) requests restricted context

expected.notes: MUST return identical response shape to a genuine not-found case. Registries MUST NOT use error_code 'visibility_denied' externally; that semantic is for internal logging only (RFC-ACDP-0008 §4.5).

scenarios.name: Unauthorized requester requests genuinely non-existent ctx_id

expected.notes: This response MUST be byte-indistinguishable from the previous scenario. Anything else (timing differences, response-size differences, error-code differences) leaks existence.

scenarios.name: Contributor (NOT in audience) requests restricted context

expected.notes: Contributors are NOT auto-authorized (T1.7). To grant a contributor read access, the producer MUST list the DID in audience explicitly. Listing a contributor in contributors alone does NOT grant read access.
